message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Context
User provided four Google Scholar XML exports (MISQ, JMIS, ISR, MS) and needs a single Markdown summary organized by research topic. All source files are read-only, and each row must be preserved unless deduplication is requested.

**User choices confirmed:**
- Classification method: Use suggested 4 topics (keyword-based)
- Deduplication: Yes, deduplicate entries by DOI/normalized title

# Goal
Produce `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/organized-scholar-entries.md` that lists every citation from the four exports grouped under the four prescribed topics, with each entry containing a linked title, authors, journal/year, DOI/PDF link, and any notes/abstract text. Record the total entry count in the header and include a verification block describing classification/deduplication choices.

# Files
- `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 MISQ.xml`
- `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 JMIS.xml`
- `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 ISR.xml`
- `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 MS.xml`

# Constraints
- Do not edit source XML files.
- Deduplicate across exports using DOI (preferred) or normalized title; report both raw and dedup counts in the Markdown summary.
- Use the provided four-topic taxonomy with reproducible keyword rules.
- Preserve the total entry count in the header.
- Write all outputs under `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/`.

# Approach
1. Parse each XML file as Excel "XML Spreadsheet 2003" format and extract all rows from the first worksheet, mapping columns by header labels (Title, Authors, Journal, Year, Abstract/Notes, DOI/Open-access URL, PDF URL, etc.).
2. Normalize each row by trimming whitespace, collapsing abstract/newline spacing, and selecting a primary URL in priority order: DOI link > PDF URL > open-access URL.
3. Deduplicate entries: use DOI (case-insensitive) as the primary key; if no DOI exists, normalize the title (lowercase, trimmed whitespace) and deduplicate on that value. Record which duplicates were collapsed so the verification block can report raw vs. unique counts.
4. Apply the prescribed topic classification by matching keywords against the title and abstract/notes text. Assign each entry to every matching topic; if none match, place it under "Other / Uncategorized".
5. Generate `organized-scholar-entries.md` with:
   - Metadata header: date, raw row count per file, total raw rows, total unique entries, classification method (keyword rules), dedup strategy, and per-topic counts.
   - Section per topic (Website Experience & Design; AI / Automation-Driven Systems; Consumer / Trust Signals; Visual / Multimedia Interactions; Other / Uncategorized). Each section opens with a brief description of the topic and its keyword triggers.
   - Bullet list entries: `- Title (preferred link) — Authors. Journal (Year). DOI: <link if available>` followed by notes/abstract/pdfs.
   - If an entry appears under multiple topics, include it in each relevant section with a parenthetical note like `(also listed under: ...)`. Include an Appendix showing canonical listing with source file names.
   - A verification block at the top summarizing counts, deduplication choice, missing DOI count, and include a spot-check table of five random entries with the keywords that triggered their topics.

# Classification rules (keyword triggers)
- **Website Experience & Design**: website, webpage, ux, "user experience", navigation, interface, layout, mobile, responsive, homepage, design
- **AI / Automation-Driven Systems**: ai, "machine learning", algorithm, automation, recommender, personalization, bot, "automated", nlp, "deep learning"
- **Consumer / Trust Signals**: trust, privacy, disclosure, reputation, review, rating, credibility, signal, "trustworthy", "information disclosure"
- **Visual / Multimedia Interactions**: image, video, visual, multimedia, ar, vr, augmented, 3d, infographic, visualisation, visualization
- **Other / Uncategorized**: entries that match none of the above keywords
Matching is case-insensitive and performed on both Title and Abstract/Notes fields.

# Verification
- Report raw row counts per XML export and aggregate total.
- Report total unique entries after deduplication.
- Report per-topic counts and note how many entries matched multiple topics.
- Flag entries missing DOI/PDF links.
- Spot-check five entries (with file/row context) and list the triggering keywords for their assigned topics.

# Deliverables
- `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/organized-scholar-entries.md` plus the verification block described above and an appendix showing canonical listing with source file tags.
- Documented classification rules and deduplication strategy within this plan file.

# Implementation steps
1. Read all four XML files to understand their structure and column names.
2. Write a Python script to parse the XML, normalize metadata, deduplicate, classify, and generate the Markdown output.
3. Run the script and produce the final Markdown file.
4. Verify counts match expected values.

# Next steps
Exit plan mode and begin implementation.


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/my/.claude/projects/-Users-my/8195acdf-05f8-431b-8f1d-9ec8329ba04b.jsonl
